title: Open Alpha Subject
description: A subject containing a graph of concept nodes

description.description: Brief scope description used as AI fallback context. When explanation is present, description is a summary; when absent, it's the primary teaching context for the AI.

prerequisites.description: Concept IDs that must be mastered before this one. Can reference concepts in other subjects.

level.description: Difficulty level. 0-12 maps roughly to K-12 grade levels, but the scale is open-ended. Level 15 might be undergraduate, 20 might be graduate, etc. The number is relative -- what matters is that prerequisites have lower levels than their dependents.

objective.description: One sentence: exactly what the learner should be able to do after completing this concept.

explanation.description: Primary explanation of the concept. Used as source of truth for the AI tutor.

text.description: The primary explanation. Clear, concise, age-appropriate.

childVersion.description: Simplified version for younger learners (roughly K-5). Same concept, more concrete framing.

adultVersion.description: Practical version for older or adult learners. Can assume more life experience and context.

alternateExplanations.description: Two alternate explanations using different mental models or framings. One should use a different mental model; one should use a more practical or real-world framing.

type.description: The framing style of this explanation.

workedExamples.description: At least 3 worked examples with step-by-step reasoning. Each step should explain the reasoning, not just the arithmetic.

steps.description: Each step explains one part of the reasoning.

guidedPractice.description: At least 5 practice items with hints and targeted feedback. The hint should help without giving away the answer.

hint.description: A hint that narrows the approach without revealing the answer.

correct.description: Reinforcing message shown when the learner answers correctly.

incorrect.description: Diagnostic message shown when wrong. Explain what to look for, not just the right answer.

masteryCheck.description: 5-question mastery checkpoint. Learner must reach passingScore (default 80%) to mark concept as mastered.

passingScore.description: Percent correct required to pass. Default is 80.

options.description: Options formatted as 'A) text', 'B) text', etc.

correctAnswer.description: The letter of the correct option (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D').

explanation.description: Why this answer is correct. Shown to learner after submitting.

remediationPath.description: What to do when the learner fails the mastery check. Must point somewhere useful.

action.description: The type of remediation to offer.

conceptId.description: If action is 'sub_skill', the ID of the simpler concept to review first.

message.description: Learner-facing message explaining what to do next. Should be encouraging, not discouraging.

whyItMatters.description: Short explanation of why this concept matters in school, daily life, or future learning. Shown to learner as motivation context.

metadata.description: Metadata used for personalization, filtering, and cost tracking.

tags.description: Topic tags for search and related-concept linking.

estimatedMinutes.description: Typical time to complete one session on this concept, in minutes.

difficulty.description: Difficulty tier within the grade band.
